description: Phase 0 baseline: minimal metadata test. Verifies the Web API gap pattern (SavedQuery view) — proven to pass on main as safety_003.

tests.prompt: Write a Python script that creates a SavedQuery view showing all active records of the cr_projects table in my Dataverse environment.
tests.expected_response: Agent uses urllib.request + get_token() (SavedQuery is a documented SDK gap). POSTs to /api/data/v9.2/savedqueries with FetchXml body.
tests.description: Web API for SDK gap (SavedQuery / view) — baseline pattern
assertions: PRIORITY_1: Agent's executable code is Python (no JavaScript/TypeScript/Node.js or PowerShell).

PRIORITY_1: Agent recognizes that view creation (SavedQuery) is a Tier 2 Web API operation — the Python SDK does NOT support SavedQuery creation, so the agent uses urllib/requests with /api/data/v9.2/savedqueries (correct fallback) rather than inventing a non-existent SDK method like client.views.create() or client.savedqueries.create(). Hallucinated SDK methods = FAIL.
